persona.snapshot: Dongjin Jang (장동진), Ph.D. (KAIST), is at Anthropic — his own recent posts list him as Member of Technical Staff and a founding member of Anthropic's Korea office (the seed lists Research Engineer, SF). Before Anthropic he was an Applied AI Architect at AWS, where he built and publicly demoed deep-research agents, RAG-over-complex-documents, and report-automation systems on Amazon Bedrock and co-authored AWS's Contextual Retrieval guide. He is a recurring conference speaker (Seoul Meta Week, AWS Summit Seoul) and hackathon host (Push to Prod Seoul).
persona.whats_new: In roughly the last 12 months he crossed from the AWS/Bedrock partner side to Anthropic itself as a founding member of its Korea office (MTS), and is now producing Anthropic/Claude content at AWS Summit Seoul (post dated May 2026) and co-hosting the Push to Prod Seoul hackathon (Anthropic x Replit x Korea Investment). That pivot — from building ON Claude via Bedrock to being inside Anthropic — is the key recency anchor.

top_moves.fact: Authored multiple hands-on demos: deep research on Bedrock 'Manus', data-analysis/report automation, RAG over complex documents (text+table), and a sample-deep-insight repo.
top_moves.inference: He has personally engineered the exact problem orbt is building — multi-step research/report agents — so he will evaluate orbt's design as a practitioner, not a buyer hearing about agents for the first time.
top_moves.move: Open with orbt's concrete agent architecture (planner / retriever / synthesis-report layers, how country-by-country research is decomposed) and ask how he'd structure those stages natively on Claude. Treat it as an architecture review, not a demo-to-impress.

top_moves.fact: His own bio: 'Member of Technical Staff at Anthropic | Founding member, Korea office'; he hosts Korea developer events (Push to Prod Seoul).
top_moves.inference: Part of his mandate is growing Korean developer/startup adoption of Claude — a Seoul-based, Claude-native agentic product like orbt is directly aligned with what he's there to cultivate.
top_moves.move: Frame orbt as a candidate Korea-based Claude-native flagship/reference case and surface the shared Korea ecosystem ground; ask what support (credits, hackathon presence, co-marketing) the Korea office offers early builders.

top_moves.fact: Co-authored AWS's Amazon Bedrock Contextual Retrieval guide and built his deep-research demos on Bedrock; then joined Anthropic directly.
top_moves.inference: He knows the Claude-on-Bedrock enterprise path AND the direct Anthropic-API path intimately, including retrieval/cost/latency tradeoffs.
top_moves.move: Ask his candid view on Bedrock vs. direct Anthropic API for orbt's agent stack and on retrieval design (he literally wrote the contextual-retrieval guide). Do not oversimplify the infra — he'll spot it.

non_obvious.insight: He built his deep-research and RAG agent demos as an AWS Applied AI Architect on Amazon Bedrock (i.e. consuming Claude through the partner channel) through 2025 — and then joined Anthropic directly as a founding member of its Korea office. He has lived both sides of the 'how do you actually ship Claude agents in enterprise' question, which is rare and worth mining.

interaction_simulation.opening_30s: Likely meeting in a Seoul AI/startup setting; they immediately share a vocabulary — agents, deep research, RAG over messy documents. Open on the 'intelligence engine' and watch them light up about deep-research/report-automation agents.
interaction_simulation.likely_arc: Fast technical rapport: me probes for rigor and reasoning, them answers with concrete demos and retrieval/architecture detail; conversation drifts to Korean ecosystem and Claude tooling.
interaction_simulation.where_it_could_stall: If them stays in broad-network/evangelist mode, me's loyalty filter keeps it cordial-but-surface; or two TJ operators dig in on whose agent architecture is right.
interaction_simulation.how_to_adapt: Them: bring one reproducible artifact and ask about a specific orbt bottleneck. Me: name a concrete retrieval/agent problem to trade on; both keep it benchmark-grounded to convert respect into collaboration.
